event.name: The CANYON Guildford Town Centre Races 2026
specialGuests: Lizzie Jordan — Paralympian gold medallist and Charlotteville CC member
Secret guest — 'one half of a famous pop duo from the 80s, and a keen cyclist'
Otto Allison (17, VC Londres, ex-Charlotteville Rascal, now racing in Europe) leads the sighting laps
event.hqNote: Race HQ, toilets & changing: Holy Trinity Church, High Street GU1 3RR — next to the finish line (NEW: different from previous years when it was the Trinity Centre). Not manned/secure.
event.signOn: Sign-on on the High Street (Charlotteville gazebo). Youth from 16:15, adults from 17:00. Closes 30 min before support races, 50 min before National Series races.

timetable.item: The Spokesman Under 8 Boys & Girls Race (2 laps) — 17:35–17:45
The Positano Under 10 & Under 12 Boys & Girls Race (15 min) — 17:55–18:10
The Geared Up Cycles Under 14 & Under 16 Open & Girls Race (20 min) — 18:15–18:35
The Foran Construction Local Heroes Open Race (Regional C+, 30 min) — 18:40–19:10
Sight lap & gridding — Women's Grand Prix
The Greyfriars Vineyard Guildford Women's Grand Prix (National A, 50 min) — 19:40–20:30
Sight lap & gridding — Open Grand Prix
The Cycle Exchange Guildford Open Grand Prix (National A, 50 min) — 20:40–21:30
Local Heroes + National Series Women's & Open prize presentations (21:30–21:45)

u8.heading: Under 8’s Circuit
paragraphs: This is the traditional circuit at Guildford. It starts on the High Street adjacent to Holy Trinity Church. The route narrows at the top of the High Street and once through this the riders make a sweeping left at the junction into North Street. There is a marshalled Crossing Point which utilises the existing pedestrian crossing just after this junction.
The route then continues along and down North Street which is tarmac with a speed table near the top.

The circuit then makes a 90deg left hand turn into Market Street (note the chamfered transition kerb) where the road narrows into a paved side street. There will be marshalled Crossing Point at this junction.

The circuit then continues up Market Street and makes another 90deg left turn into the cobbled High Street. There is a marshalled Crossing Point at this junction. The circuit then continues up the High Street with the finish line outside Holy Trinity Church.

extended.heading: Extended Circuit U10 – U16, Local Heroes and National Circuit Series Races
paragraphs: The circuit starts on the High Street adjacent to Holy Trinity Church. The route narrows at the top of the High Street and once through this the riders bear right at the junction and continue up the upper High Street A3100 on the right-hand side of the carriageway. The circuit continues to the upper end of the High Street where it does a U-turn prior to the speed table adjacent to Pizza Express (the Pits are located here).

The circuit then continues back down Upper High Street, on the “wrong side of the road” before making a 90deg right hand turn next to Rymans into North Street (note the kerb and fast nature of this corner). There will be no Crossing Points in this section due to the speed the riders will be moving. Pedestrians will be able to cross around the circuit where it performed a U-turn.
The route then continues along and down North Street which is tarmac with a speed table near the top.
The circuit then makes a 90deg left hand turn into the paved Market Street (note the chamfered transition kerb) where the road narrows. There will be marshalled Crossing Point at this junction.

The circuit then continues up Market Street and makes another 90deg left turn into the cobbled High Street. There is a marshalled Crossing Point at this junction. The circuit then continues up the High Street with the finish line outside Holy Trinity Church.
extended.extraText: THERE ARE NO LAPS OUT. If you have a mechanical issue then you can receive service from the pits only – which are located at the U-Turn on the “out & back” section of the circuit. The pits are only available for the National Series Races.

elite.pits: NO LAPS OUT — pits only, at the U-turn on the out-and-back section (National Series races only).
elite.gridding: After sighting lap: i. Current National Circuit Race Champion · ii. Previous event winner · iii. National Series Leader · iv. Sprints Competition Leader · v. Top 20 of series standings (2nd–20th in order) · vi. All other riders
elite.sprints: Two sprints per round toward the sprint classification, after approx. 15 and 30 minutes, indicated at one lap to go. Points 10-8-6-4-2 for top 5. Riders must be placed at the finish to keep sprint points.
elite.lapped: Lapped riders continue until 3 laps to go, sprint for position, then withdraw via the Pit Area. Never re-cross the finish line when retiring.
elite.blueFlag: Final 3 laps: riders about to be lapped shown a blue flag — keep tight left. If lapped inside the final 3 laps, continue to contest the finish on the final lap.
elite.prizes: £2,000 per National Series race, shared across the first 20 places per series regulations. Podium: first 3 in full team clothing at the BC podium truck. BC will also present series leader & sprint competition jerseys, plus the winning national series team on the night.
elite.incidents: Black flag = race stopped; red chequered flag = neutralised. Gaps may be reinstated using timing data.

support.distance: U8: 2 laps · U10/U12: 15 min · U14/U16: 20 min · Local Heroes: 30 min (Regional C+)
support.lapped: Support race riders will be allowed to finish even if lapped — keep off the racing line.
support.prizes: Youth: first 3 in each category (U8 Girls & Boys, U10 Girls & Boys, U12 Girls & Boys, U14 Girls & Open, U16 Girls & Open) at 19:10. Local Heroes podium at 21:30 with the elite races.
support.signOn: Youth sign-on from 16:15; closes 30 min before each support race.

sponsors.name: CANYON
sponsors.blurb:
Canyon is a leading direct-to-consumer bicycle brand known for its innovative design, engineering excellence and uncompromising performance. Founded in Germany, Canyon offers a diverse range of high-quality bikes — from road and mountain to gravel and e-bikes — meticulously crafted to meet the needs of cyclists at every level. Selling directly to consumers ensures exceptional value and service, and keeps Canyon closely connected with the cycling community worldwide, pushing the limits of technology and design so riders can achieve their best, whether racing, exploring or commuting.

Title sponsor of the 2026 Guildford Town Centre Races and one of the world's biggest bicycle manufacturers — look out for their display stands around the circuit.

sponsors.name: Cycle Exchange
sponsors.blurb:
Since 2013, Cycle Exchange have been buying premium used bikes from cyclists — mainly performance road bikes, but also gravel, TT, mountain and e-bikes. Every bike is serviced, refurbished and fully warrantied before sale, so it's like buying new.

Specialist retailer of pre-owned and ex-demo bikes — buy, sell and trade quality second-hand machines. Sponsors the Cycle Exchange Guildford Open Grand Prix.

sponsors.name: Greyfriars Vineyard
sponsors.blurb:
Greyfriars Vineyard sits on the sunny, south-facing chalk slopes of the Hog's Back at Puttenham, just outside Guildford in Surrey, producing a range of spectacular English sparkling and still wines that reflect the unique local soil conditions, climate and heritage of the Surrey North Downs.

First planted in 1989 — one of England's early Champagne-variety vineyards (Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, Pinot Meunier). Run by Mike & Hilary Wagstaff since 2011; 40 acres, a modern winery and underground chalk cellar, and multiple Decanter World Wine Award winners.

sponsors.name: Foran Construction
sponsors.blurb:
At Foran Construction, the emphasis is on building lasting, trusting relationships with clients — treating every project on its own merit and truly understanding each client's needs, with a commitment to high standards of service, quality and customer satisfaction. Whether you need building contractors, are inviting tenders, want a complete design-and-build solution, or need help improving your business premises, Foran Construction can assist.

Backer of Foran Cycling Team, a SW London-based elite squad founded in 2018 and known for its rider-first culture (supported by Sigma Sports, Spatzwear, Precision Hydration). Guildford is one of the team's key race targets, and Foran CT riders line up in the Open GP tonight. Title sponsor of the Local Heroes Race.

sponsors.name: The Spokesman
sponsors.blurb:
At The Spokesman, the workshop is at the heart of everything they do. A well-maintained bike is key to a great ride, so they offer expert services for all types of bicycles — from road and gravel bikes to mountain bikes and e-bikes — with skilled technicians using years of experience and the latest tools for high-quality repairs, tune-ups and custom servicing.

The Spokesman Bicycle Workshop is based at Home Farm, Albury, on the edge of Guildford in the Surrey Hills. Established in 2014 as a mobile service, with the workshop opening in 2016; walk-in repairs, no booking system, 4.9-star rated. Title sponsor of the Under 8 race.

sponsors.name: Positano
sponsors.blurb:
The first thing you'll notice entering Positano Ristorante Italiano is the warm, welcoming atmosphere — you're among friends here, enjoying the best traditional Italian dishes with a glass of Italian wine. The attractively decorated dining rooms are steeped in history and provide a superb backdrop for memorable meals. Franco, Cosmin and Simone invite you to share your dining experience with them, whether it's a romantic dinner for two, a business lunch, corporate function, birthday celebration or a meal with the family.

Italian restaurant in Tunsgate, Guildford town centre — check them out just off the race circuit. Title sponsor of the U10 & U12 race.

sponsors.name: Geared Up Cycles
sponsors.blurb:
Geared Up Cycles have over 20 years of experience in the cycling industry and aim to take the hassle out of maintaining your bike, always striving for short lead times — same-day slots sometimes available. They offer new bikes from Merida, Ridgeback & Genesis, alongside an ever-increasing range of spare parts and accessories.

Local bike shop established in 2010 by Steve Groombridge, based at Sutton Green Garden Centre between Guildford and Woking, with a second workshop at the University of Surrey in Guildford. Sales, spares, repairs and servicing; 5-star rated. Title sponsor of the U14 & U16 race.

sponsors.name: Arch & Bloom (Berkeley Group)
sponsors.blurb:
Launching 11 July 2026: set within 6.6 acres in the heart of Guildford, Arch & Bloom brings a timeless collection of studio, 1, 2 and 3-bedroom homes to a vibrant new neighbourhood. Lively cafés, curated shops and well-considered private residents' amenities — including concierge, gym, cinema and co-working space — shape a place designed for connection, ease and everyday enjoyment.

High Street location within walking distance of Surrey University, close to the Surrey Hills Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Berkeley Group's new residential development on North Street, Guildford — right on the race circuit; visit their stand to find out about the scheme, or see the Berkeley Homes website for more.

sponsors.name: Rethink Your IT
sponsors.blurb:
Rethink Your IT spends its days working with businesses across Guildford and Surrey, so it's delighted to support an event that brings so many people together in the heart of town. They love helping businesses get more from their technology — taking care of day-to-day IT support, improving security and providing practical advice, so owners spend less time worrying about technology and more time running their businesses.

Local Guildford IT support company run by Liz Turner, supporting small businesses (1–50 people) across Surrey with cybersecurity, Microsoft 365 and practical AI advice — plain language, no jargon. Helping businesses stay secure, productive & AI-ready: rethinkyourit.co.uk · hello@rethinkyourit.co.uk

sponsors.name: SunGod
sponsors.blurb:
At SunGod, they see a better world — through their lenses, through their actions and through doing business better. Their planet is their playground, so SunGod is a community-built brand that's sustainable to its core: together, the mission is to See Better.

Performance eyewear brand — offering riders 20% off with code GUILDFORD-CRIT-20 (valid until 11:30pm, Wed 22 July 2026).

sponsors.name: Bell & Spoke
sponsors.blurb:
Bell & Spoke is a built environment consultancy working with public and private sector clients on projects that improve the health and wellbeing of communities. Services include bid support, project development, and client & project sponsor advice, with sector experience spanning active travel and cycling, education, healthcare, infrastructure, urban regeneration, residential, sustainability and zero carbon.

Founded by Austin Bell, who brings over 30 years' experience in the construction industry on both the client and contractor side, and has led JV and PPP bid teams securing projects worth over £4bn of revenue, predominantly in the public sector.

sponsors.name: Experience Guildford
sponsors.blurb: Town-centre business partnership — check the Experience Guildford app for offers available in town on race night.

history.narrative: The Guildford Town Centre Races date back over four decades — 2026 is the 42nd edition, organised throughout by Charlotteville Cycling Club (founded 1903). The race returned to the National Circuit Series calendar in 2023 with the extended 1km circuit taking in the cobbled High Street, North Street and Market Street.

pastWinners.note: Winners taken from the parsed results archive (2023 from The British Continental report; no 2023 youth/Local Heroes results in our sources; no event in 2020).

defendingChampions.open: Milo Wills won the 2025 Open GP for TEKKERZ CC — he lines up tonight at bib 1, now riding for camsmajaco.
defendingChampions.women: Robyn Clay (DAS-Hutchinson) won the 2025 Women's GP — not on tonight's startlist (now WorldTour with Team Picnic PostNL, 4th at the 2026 Nat Circuit Champs).
defendingChampions.heroes: Miles Horner (IGNITE Race Team) won the 2025 Local Heroes race. 2024 winner Max Duckworth has moved up — he rides tonight's Open GP at bib 47 for O'Neills Spirit Racing Team.

ncs.name: Lloyds National Circuit Series 2026
ncs.scoring: Top 40 score: 50, 48, 46… (top 11 at −2/place), then 29, 28… (places 12–40 at −1/place). Team classification from each team's top 3 finishers per round. Sprints classification: 10-8-6-4-2 at two sprints per round.
